2026 will be less about features and more about posture in the clinical space."},{"url":"https://www.index.dev/blog/perplexity-ai-features-statistics","title":"Perplexity AI Features and Statistics 2026: What You Can Actually Do","content":"This report covers its traffic, users, accuracy, revenue, 2026 model lineup, and the free Comet browser, plus what it all means for hiring AI engineers.
In 2026 Perplexity has Pro Search for deep research, Spaces for organizing work, a Model Council that compares answers from several models side by side, and the free Comet browser that reached number three on the US App Store within 48 hours of its iOS launch.
The platform reports over 100 million monthly active users across all products in 2026, per Sacra’s April 2026 analysis, and now carries a ~$20 billion valuation after a $200 million round in September 2025.
Pro Search reads 300+ sources per query and the new Model Council compares GPT-5.2, Claude 4.6, and Gemini 3.1 Pro side by side.
Here is what you get with Perplexity right now.
Memory and personalization: remembers past threads and interests to improve context and answers across models.
Max plan: full enterprise features including the Background Assistant and Comet Plus (about $200 per month)
Germany, South Korea, and Japan all show a strong preference for desktop access, with over 87% of users on computers.
Perplexity partnered with Telkomsel in May 2025.
Comet launched free worldwide in October 2025.
In 2026 Pro and Max users pick from a curated set of frontier models, and a new Model Council compares them side by side.
Long creative threads: conversational memory across a long session is weaker than ChatGPT.
Gemini 3.1 Pro: long context and fast responses
Max file size: 25MB on Pro
Perplexity lets you use multiple models (GPT, Claude, Gemini all in one place)"}]}
